Document:
# Survivor Roleplay Standards
## General - Survivors must accept requests/invitations to board the Almayer for the first time, but are not required to stay after they arrive. Make sure to follow the direction and requests of the Military Police.
## Hostility to Marines is Not Permitted - Survivors have more leeway than marines with mental stability due to trauma experienced during the xenonid attack.\nThey may be suffering PTSD, paranoia or similar conditions for roleplaying purposes.\n\nDespite this, all survivors may not become hostile or cause drastic issues for the marines in any way, such as stealing their secure equipment, starting verbal or physical fights, or preventing them from accessing any part of the operation.
[bold]Exceptions[/bold] - If there is an exception in hostility due to faction it will be clear and written to the player. Do not assume that a survivor role is hostile for any other reason. - Hostile Faction Survivors cannot attempt to harm other survivors.
## Metagaming/Powergaming/Etc
- Do not hunt, move, or secure or otherwise interact with mapped animals that can be captured.\nThese creatures reduce the amount of xenonids at the start of the round.
- Survivors may not complete marine objectives, such as collecting intel related objects, until they are rescued or have been enlisted into the marines.\nThis does not include repairing colony assets, such as power or communications, or recovering corpses using roleplay reasoning while doing so.
